title: The Alternate Route — Hargrove's Marked Map
description: Hargrove's map, if examined or if he is persuaded to share it, shows a secondary path through the bluff grass that bypasses the wire obstacle entirely — a goat track used by local farmers that the German fortification plan did not fully cover. It is narrow, it can only be used by men on foot in single file, and it bypasses the draw rather than opening it — meaning armor cannot follow. But it gets men to the top of the bluff, which changes the tactical equation at WN-71 from the rear.
